summary: Iteration 148: Invitation Onboarding Flow Refonte - All tests PASSED (11/13 backend, 2 skipped). Verified new /link-account and /link-user endpoints work correctly. Frontend InvitationAccountChoice component properly calls linkAccount. Account creation does NOT auto-accept invitation (status remains 'invited'). Token stored as nlyt_token. Scenario C (logged-in user with email mismatch) correctly stays on invitation page.

integration_issues.flow: Skip account flow on past appointment
integration_issues.issue: Error 'body stream already read' shown when skipping account on an appointment that has already started. This is a minor error handling issue - the backend correctly returns 400 but frontend error parsing fails.

backend_api_tests.test_link_account_new_user_creates_accou: PASS - Creates account, returns JWT, is_new_account=true

frontend_tests.invitation_page_loads: PASS - Page loads with invitation card
frontend_tests.existing_account_banner: PASS - Banner shown for existing accounts
frontend_tests.accept_button_triggers_account_choice: PASS - Clicking Accept shows account choice panel
frontend_tests.account_choice_panel_elements: PASS - Password input, submit button, skip link all present
frontend_tests.display_name_and_email: PASS - Shows participant name and email
frontend_tests.scenario_c_email_mismatch: PASS - Logged-in user with different email stays on invitation page

critical_code_review_comments: Backend /link-account endpoint correctly creates account without accepting invitation
Backend /link-user endpoint correctly validates email match (403 on mismatch)
Frontend AuthContext.loginWithToken properly sets nlyt_token and nlyt_user
Frontend api.js has linkAccount and linkUser methods in invitationAPI
Token storage uses nlyt_token key consistently across codebase

success_rate.backend: 100% (11/11 tests passed, 2 skipped due to test data constraints)

seed_data_creation: None - used existing test invitation token
context_for_next_testing_agent: Invitation onboarding flow refonte complete. New endpoints /link-account and /link-user work correctly. The flow is: 1) User clicks Accept on invitation page, 2) Account choice panel appears, 3) User enters password to create account or login, 4) Frontend calls /link-account which returns JWT + user data + is_new_account flag, 5) Frontend calls loginWithToken to store auth, 6) User is redirected to /dashboard. The invitation status remains 'invited' until user explicitly accepts from dashboard (which may trigger Stripe guarantee flow if penalty > 0). Scenario C (logged-in user visiting invitation link) auto-redirects to dashboard only if email matches.

scenario_a_new_user.behavior: Creates account, returns JWT, is_new_account=true, status stays 'invited'

scenario_b_existing_user.behavior: Validates password, returns JWT, is_new_account=false, status stays 'invited'

scenario_c_logged_in_user.behavior: Links user_id to participant if email matches, returns 403 if mismatch

dashboard_action_required.behavior: Returns invited participant items in action_required bucket with Accept/Decline buttons
